pi_items.source: Task instructions and setup_task.sh explicitly state the output path.
Calculated from the total bounds of the ne_110m_admin_0_countries shapefile (-180 to 180 degrees X), spanning exactly 360 degrees.
Calculated from the total bounds of the ne_110m_admin_0_countries shapefile (-90 to 83.6 degrees Y), which gives an exact height of ~174 degrees, slightly contradicting the approximation of 180.
Code execution on the ne_110m_admin_0_countries shapefile confirms the minimum value of the 'MAPCOLOR7' attribute is exactly 1.
Code execution on the ne_110m_admin_0_countries shapefile confirms the maximum value of the 'MAPCOLOR7' attribute is exactly 7.
Task instructions explicitly state to set the Cell size / Resolution to 1.0.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires converting the Natural Earth countries shapefile to a raster grid using gvSIG's Geoprocessing tools. The input data is real, public-domain Natural Earth data. The expected output file path is '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/country_grid.tif' as specified in the instructions. The target resolution is explicitly set to 1.0 degree. The bounding box of the input shapefile spans exactly 360 degrees in width (-180 to 180) and 173.6 degrees in height (-90 to 83.6), resulting in an expected raster size of 360x174 pixels at 1.0 resolution, which slightly contradicts the metadata approximation of 180 pixels. The MAPCOLOR7 attribute used for raster values has been verified by directly querying the dataset, confirming a range exactly from 1 to 7.
